Ruling - ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows

DEFENDANT FCA US LLC, LITHIA OF CONCORD I, INC., MOTION TO TAX COSTS

The motion to tax is granted only as to the other travel costs and deny in all other respects. Counsel for the plaintiff to send in an order that is approved as to form and that complies with CRC 3.1312 and send the order in this morning.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600 email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LEONARD SHAVIES' Motion For Attorneys Fees.

Plaintiff Shavies' motion for recovery of attorney's fees is GRANTED. Shavies is entitled to reasonable attorney's fees as the prevailing part in this Song-Beverly Act action. (CC 1794(d).) The court determines that the Knight Law Group's use of numerous lawyers to litigate this lemon law case was excessive and reduce its fees by 20% (Total recovery = $10,560). The court determines that Mr. Rosenstein's claimed fees are reasonable except that he should not recover $1,650 for traveling to San Francisco for trial call after this case settled. Rosenstein should recover $38,962.60. The court further finds that based on the contingent nature of this case, its difficulty and plaintiff's success, Shavies should recover a multiplier of .2 Shavies therefore is entitled to a total of $59,427.12. This motion does not address Shavies' request for costs.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
